name: Zai Lab Ltd
business: Zai Lab is a biopharmaceutical company focused on discovering, developing, and commercializing products in oncology, immunology, neuroscience, and infectious disease. It has seven commercial programs with approved products launched in Greater China. The company sells its products to distributors who sell to healthcare providers, primarily in mainland China.

exposures.sentence: We are a patient-focused, innovative, commercial-stage, global biopharmaceutical company with a substantial presence in both Greater China and the United States.
OPTUNE is a cancer therapy that uses electric fields tuned to specific frequencies to kill tumor cells via a variety of mechanisms. TTFields therapy is delivered through a portable medical device.

catalysts.event: NMPA approved sNDA for AUGTYRO for NTRK+ solid tumors
catalysts.sentence: In December 2025, the NMPA approved the sNDA for AUGTYRO for the treatment of adult patients with NTRK + solid tumors.

catalysts.event: NMPA approved NDA for KarXT for schizophrenia
catalysts.sentence: in December 2025, the NMPA approved the NDA for KarXT for the treatment of schizophrenia in adults.

catalysts.event: Hong Kong Department of Health approved TIVDAK for cervical cancer
catalysts.sentence: In September 2025, the Hong Kong Department of Health approved TIVDAK in Hong Kong for the treatment of adult patients with recurrent or metastatic cervical cancer with disease progression on or after chemotherapy.

catalysts.event: FDA granted Orphan Drug Designation to zoci for SCLC
catalysts.sentence: In January 2025, the FDA granted Orphan Drug Designation to zoci as a treatment for patients with SCLC.

catalysts.event: FDA granted Fast Track Designation to zoci for SCLC
catalysts.sentence: in May 2025, the FDA granted Fast Track Designation for this indication.

catalysts.event: NMPA accepted BLA for TIVDAK for cervical cancer
catalysts.sentence: in March 2025, the NMPA accepted the BLA for TIVDAK for the treatment of patients with recurrent or metastatic cervical cancer with disease progression on or after systemic therapy.

catalysts.event: FDA approved TTFields for pancreatic cancer (OPTUNE Pax)
catalysts.sentence: In February 2026, the FDA approved TTFields, under the brand name OPTUNE Pax, for this indication.

catalysts.event: Filing for regulatory approval of TTFields for pancreatic cancer in China
catalysts.sentence: We filed for regulatory approval in China in the fourth quarter of 2025.

catalysts.event: Expected commercial launch of KarXT in China
catalysts.sentence: We expect to commercially launch KarXT in China in the first half of 2026.

catalysts.event: China Schizophrenia Prevention and Treatment Guidelines included KarXT
catalysts.sentence: In September 2025, the China Schizophrenia Prevention and Treatment Guidelines (2025 Edition) were officially released, and KarXT was included for the first time.

tamClaims.claim: Over 61,100 newly diagnosed ovarian cancer cases and 32,600 deaths annually in China
tamClaims.sentence: Ovarian cancer is one of the most common gynecological cancers in China, with over 61,100 newly diagnosed cases and 32,600 deaths in China annually.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 200,000 patients in China living with Myasthenia Gravis
tamClaims.sentence: There are approximately 200,000 patients in China living with MG.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 50,000 patients diagnosed with CIDP in mainland China
tamClaims.sentence: There are approximately 50,000 patients diagnosed with CIDP in mainland China.

tamClaims.claim: Estimated incidence of CABP in mainland China approximately 10 million patients
tamClaims.sentence: In 2020, the estimated incidence of CABP in mainland China was approximately 10 million patients

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 134,000 new pancreatic cancer cases per year in China
tamClaims.sentence: There are approximately 134,000 new cases of pancreatic cancer diagnosed each year in China.

tamClaims.claim: Estimated annual incidence of 30,000 newly diagnosed GIST patients per year in mainland China
tamClaims.sentence: with an estimated annual incidence of around 30,000 newly diagnosed patients per year in mainland China.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 1.1 million new lung cancer cases in China in 2022
tamClaims.sentence: In China, there were approximately 1.1 million new cases of lung cancer in 2022.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately three to five million patients with IgAN in China
tamClaims.sentence: There are approximately three to five million patients with IgAN in China, including approximately 750,000 already diagnosed with the disease.
